SEARCH
Package textadventure.entities

Class Room

java.lang.Object
textadventure.entities.Room
public class Room extends Object
The Room class represents a room in the game world. A room has a name, a description, a list of items contained within it, and exits that lead to other rooms.

Each room can contain multiple items, and the player can interact with these items. The room also has exits that allow the player to move between rooms, forming the navigation system of the game.

Author:
qaffqa

  • Constructor Details

    • Room

      public Room(String name, String description)
      Constructs a new Room object with a given name and description. Initializes an empty list for items and an empty map for exits.
      Parameters:
      name - The name of the room.
      description - A brief description of the room.

  • Method Details

    • getName

      public String getName()
      Gets the name of the room.
      Returns:
      The name of the room.

    • getDescription

      public String getDescription()
      Gets the description of the room.
      Returns:
      The description of the room.

    • getItems

      public List<Item> getItems()
      Gets the list of items in the room.
      Returns:
      A list of items in the room.

    • addItem

      public void addItem(Item item)
      Adds an item to the room.
      Parameters:
      item - The item to add to the room.

    • removeItem

      public void removeItem(Item item)
      Removes an item from the room.
      Parameters:
      item - The item to remove from the room.

    • setExits

      public void setExits(String direction, Room room)
      Sets an exit from this room to another room in the specified direction.
      Parameters:
      direction - The direction of the exit (e.g., "north", "south").
      room - The room to which the exit leads.

    • getExit

      public Room getExit(String direction)
      Gets the room in the specified direction.
      Parameters:
      direction - The direction of the exit (e.g., "north", "south").
      Returns:
      The room in the specified direction, or null if no exit exists.

    • getExits

      public String getExits()
      Returns a string representation of all exits from the room.
      Returns:
      A string containing all exit directions.
